If there are joyous events such as marriage, childbirth, property purchase, or starting a business in the Year of the Horse, the negative impact of Tai Sui can be slightly mitigated. Otherwise, they need to be prepared for many troubles. Although things may start smoothly, there will be difficulties and setbacks along the way. "Bao Bai" means that financial fortune will fluctuate greatly, especially for business people. bornYear.reading: In the Year of the Horse, financial losses are easily incurred due to being deceived or making poor decisions. Therefore, if younger relatives ask to lend money, think carefully before acting. If you still intend to help them, be prepared that accepting such assistance may be irreversible. In terms of investment, a conservative approach is advised; avoid high-risk speculative transactions. Even when playing mahjong or other forms of gambling, keep it to small stakes to avoid significant risks. Regarding health, the strong fire energy requires special attention to heart and blood pressure. Fortunately, overall health is not significantly affected, and harmonious family relationships will help maintain a cheerful mood. Therefore, the Year of the Horse is still considered a lucky year.

I'm currently experiencing some anxiety, often overthinking and worrying about unnecessary things. Fortunately, my actual fortune is quite good, so I suggest going for walks or spending time in nature more often. I could also try developing some hobbies, such as gardening or raising fish. Meeting up with friends for tea can also help maintain a positive social atmosphere and improve my mood. My family's fortune is experiencing a slight impact, possibly due to noise or leaks; therefore, I recommend seeking professional repairs. Regarding health, my stomach and digestive system are relatively weak, so I should keep my diet light to avoid foodborne illnesses.

The Year of the Horse brings the influence of the "Bing-Xin combination" in the year pillar, meaning more trivial matters will require careful handling within the family, such as noise issues, leaks, or electrical malfunctions. These need to be repaired and addressed promptly. Regarding the younger generation, we shouldn't interfere too much, but rather allow them the freedom to develop their potential. We ourselves can choose to spend more time in nature and take short trips, which will undoubtedly strengthen our relationships. In terms of health, special attention must be paid to the throat, trachea, and respiratory tract. Those with nasal allergies should pay particular attention to air quality to prevent allergens in the living environment from worsening their health. Regarding investments, the best approach is to focus on small investments rather than large bets, and to avoid joint investments with others to prevent disagreements and disharmony.

In terms of mindset, there's often a pessimistic undertone, coupled with a quick temper, making it easy to vent negative emotions on others. Therefore, it's necessary to adjust one's mindset and maintain a positive outlook. Additionally, participating in more sports or spending more time in nature can help absorb more positive energy. Financially, there will be some improvement compared to the Year of the Snake, with gains in both regular and windfall income. However, it's still a year of conflict with Tai Sui, so high-risk speculative activities should be avoided. Health-wise, due to the clash between water and fire, special attention should be paid to heart problems, high blood pressure, and common urban diseases like hypertension, hyperlipidemia, and diabetes. Therefore, strengthening health management and regular checkups are necessary to ensure safety. In the Year of the Horse, it's also important to care for the health of the elderly; consider repairing their homes or replacing furniture, as these actions can help improve overall fortune.

Individuals who acquire wealth through professional skills or eloquence may thrive, but they are prone to disputes. Therefore, it's best to avoid expressing opinions on matters that don't concern them. Furthermore, they should avoid acting as mediators to resolve conflicts to prevent being attacked. In fact, those born in the year of Yi Chou (乙丑) are traditionally considered to be in a "year of misfortune," inevitably facing significant fluctuations in fortune. It is advised that they slow down, adopt a "letting go" mentality, avoid large-scale investments or major decisions, and instead spend more time on further education to enhance their personal value. It would be more prudent to embark on a new journey after their fortune stabilizes.

During the Year of the Horse, various reasons may lead to unexpected financial losses. Increased social activities and frequent entertainment will result in additional expenses, so it is advisable to manage finances more carefully to prevent overspending. Furthermore, business owners must pay attention to increasing revenue and reducing expenditure, and build up capital reserves for unforeseen circumstances. They should also monitor their clients' financial situations and avoid lending or extending credit to prevent defaults from impacting their own cash flow. For those already in relationships, the stability of the relationship is generally good, but be wary of the "critical year" of the Year of the Sheep, which may cause changes. If your relationship is solid, you can consider taking it further. Single individuals will have the opportunity to expand their social circle, although even if they meet potential partners, these encounters are usually brief and unlikely to lead to long-term, stable relationships. Therefore, it is not advisable to invest emotionally too early.

Overall, your studies are progressing well, and your academic performance has improved. However, it's important not to put too much pressure on yourself. Adjust your mindset and calmly face challenges. When participating in extracurricular activities, it's best to choose one or two projects that interest you and focus on developing them, rather than randomly expanding your participation to avoid wasting too much time and affecting your concentration. In the Year of the Horse, you may have the opportunity to gain the favor of elders, but differences in perspectives between generations are inevitable. Therefore, honest communication is necessary to resolve disagreements. In the new year, be mindful of the risk of joint injuries and exercise extra caution when participating in outdoor activities.

Children born in the Year of the Ox (Xin Chou year) typically possess sharp minds and fluent speech, showing significant improvement in both their studies and knowledge absorption, thus receiving extra care from teachers and parents. However, due to the mutual influence of their birth year pillars, they may be more prone to injury, so special attention must be paid to potential accidents in the home environment, such as in the kitchen or bathroom. Additionally, their throats and tracheas are relatively fragile, making them susceptible to chronic coughs that are difficult to cure. Therefore, it is recommended that they participate in sports training such as swimming or running to strengthen their lung health through appropriate physical activity. Since overall health is relatively average in the Year of the Horse, it is advised to avoid placing the head under the influence of illness stars when washing hair; if necessary, this can be mitigated by using copper utensils or other heavy objects.
